manus.name: Manus (Meta)
manus.summary: Embedded inside Meta Ads Manager since Feb 2026. Handles report building, audience research, competitor analysis. Already in your ad account — no setup.
manus.best_for: Meta advertisers who want automated reporting and competitive intel
manus.limitations: Meta-only, currently prone to hallucination, opaque data layer
manus.starter_prompt: Show me a performance breakdown of my top 5 ad sets this month, including cost per result and ROAS. Flag any that are underperforming compared to last month.
getting_started_steps: Open Meta Ads Manager (link above)
Look for the AI assistant icon in the top navigation
Click it and paste the prompt below
Review the generated report and ask follow-up questions
suggested_prompts: Analyze my ad spend this month and flag underperforming campaigns
Research my top 3 competitors' ad strategies
Build a weekly performance report I can share with my team
Suggest audience segments I should test based on my best-performing ads

claude_cowork.name: Claude Cowork
claude_cowork.summary: Sandboxed desktop agent for document and file automation. You define a folder, describe an outcome, it executes multi-step tasks. Built by Anthropic.
claude_cowork.best_for: Knowledge workers automating document-heavy workflows without touching a terminal
claude_cowork.limitations: No persistent memory between sessions, no messaging app integration, requires Pro/Max plan
claude_cowork.starter_prompt: Go through the files in this folder and create a summary document that lists each file's purpose, key findings, and any action items mentioned. Output as a single markdown file.
getting_started_steps: Download Claude Desktop (link above)
Sign up for Claude Pro or Max
Open Claude Desktop and select a folder to work with
Paste the prompt below and describe your task
Review the output and iterate
suggested_prompts: Summarize all the documents in this folder into a one-page brief
Extract action items from these meeting notes and organize by owner
Reformat these reports into a consistent template
Compare these two contracts and highlight the differences

openclaw.name: OpenClaw
openclaw.summary: Open-source 24/7 agent running on your machine or a VPS. Communicates via WhatsApp, Telegram, Slack, iMessage. Persistent memory across weeks. Model-agnostic.
openclaw.best_for: Always-on personal or business automation, cross-app life OS, technical users
openclaw.limitations: Real security risks (prompt injection, malicious community skills), requires Docker/VPS setup
openclaw.starter_prompt: Every morning at 8am, check my calendar for today's meetings, summarize any prep materials, and send me a briefing via Slack with agenda items and key context for each meeting.
getting_started_steps: Visit the GitHub repo (link above)
Follow the Docker setup guide in the README
Connect your messaging platform (Slack, Telegram, etc.)
Configure your API keys for your preferred AI model
Send it the prompt below via your connected messenger
suggested_prompts: Monitor my email for meeting invites and auto-add prep notes to my calendar
Every Friday, compile a weekly summary of my Slack channels and send it to me
When I message you a contact name, pull their latest interactions from my CRM
Track my team's standup messages and flag any blockers to me immediately

notion_custom_agents.name: Notion Custom Agents
notion_custom_agents.summary: Agent layer inside Notion that pulls workspace context and runs multi-step tasks across connected integrations.
notion_custom_agents.best_for: Teams already living in Notion who want agentic workflows without leaving the ecosystem
notion_custom_agents.limitations: Notion Business plan required, degrades at 10k+ rows, limited to Notion ecosystem
notion_custom_agents.starter_prompt: Review my project tracker database and identify any tasks that are overdue or at risk. For each one, draft a follow-up message to the assignee asking for a status update.
getting_started_steps: Make sure you're on a Notion Business plan
Go to Settings > Agents in your Notion workspace
Create a new custom agent
Give it access to your relevant databases
Paste the prompt below as the agent's first instruction
suggested_prompts: Scan my project tracker for overdue tasks and draft follow-up messages
Create a weekly status report from my team's task updates
When a new item is added to the inbox database, triage and assign it
Summarize all meeting notes from this week and extract action items

claude_code.name: Claude Code
claude_code.summary: Terminal-native coding agent by Anthropic. Deep code reasoning, best-in-class for multi-file refactors. Lives in your terminal or IDE.
claude_code.best_for: Developers doing complex code work, refactoring, or building agentic applications
claude_code.limitations: Developer-only, no persistent memory between sessions
claude_code.cost: Included with Claude Max ($100/mo) or API
claude_code.starter_prompt: Explore this codebase and give me a high-level architecture overview. Then identify the top 3 areas where code quality could be improved, with specific suggestions.
getting_started_steps: Install Claude Code: npm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code
Subscribe to Claude Max ($100/mo) or set up API billing
Open your terminal in your project directory
Run 'claude' to start a session
Paste the prompt below
suggested_prompts: Explore this repo and explain the architecture
Refactor this module to use dependency injection
Write tests for all untested functions in src/
Find and fix all security vulnerabilities in this codebase

codex.name: Codex (OpenAI)
codex.summary: Cloud-first coding agent by OpenAI. Strong on long-horizon planning and parallel subtasks.
codex.best_for: Developers in the OpenAI ecosystem, complex multi-file architectures
codex.limitations: Cloud-only, local CLI lags Claude Code on latency
codex.starter_prompt: Analyze this repository structure and create a detailed plan to add a REST API layer. Include routing, middleware, error handling, and tests.
getting_started_steps: Subscribe to ChatGPT Pro
Go to chatgpt.com/codex (link above)
Connect your GitHub repository
Paste the prompt below to start a task
Review the generated PR when complete
suggested_prompts: Add comprehensive test coverage to this project
Refactor the database layer to use connection pooling
Create a CI/CD pipeline for this project
Migrate this codebase from JavaScript to TypeScript

perplexity_computer.name: Perplexity Computer
perplexity_computer.summary: General-purpose digital worker that routes tasks across 19 AI models. Designed for long-running research and cross-app work.
perplexity_computer.best_for: Deep research, competitive intelligence, cross-platform tasks where you want the best model per subtask
perplexity_computer.limitations: Plans start at $200+/mo with additional AI credits
perplexity_computer.starter_prompt: Research the top 10 competitors in [your industry]. For each, compile: pricing model, key features, recent product launches, and estimated market share. Output as a comparison table.
getting_started_steps: Sign up at perplexity.ai (link above)
Subscribe to a plan with Computer access ($200+/mo)
Open a new Computer session
Paste the prompt below (replace [your industry] with yours)
Let it run — this can take 10-30 minutes for deep research
suggested_prompts: Research our top 10 competitors and build a comparison matrix
Find all recent funding announcements in [industry] from the last 30 days
Compile a market landscape report with pricing, features, and positioning
Monitor these 5 company blogs and summarize any product updates weekly

cofounder.name: Cofounder
cofounder.summary: Natural language automation platform that connects 80+ tools. Describe what you want in plain English and it builds the workflow. Persistent memory that learns your business over time.
cofounder.best_for: Founders and ops leaders who want to automate cross-tool workflows without writing code
cofounder.limitations: Credit-based usage can add up for heavy use, newer platform with smaller community than alternatives
cofounder.starter_prompt: Connect to my Gmail, Slack, and calendar. Every morning at 8am, send me a briefing in Slack with today's meetings, any emails that need a response, and my top 3 priorities from yesterday's unfinished tasks.
getting_started_steps: Sign up at app.cofounder.co (link above)
Connect your key tools (Gmail, Slack, calendar, etc.)
Describe your first automation in plain English
Review and approve the workflow it creates
Let it run — it learns and improves over time
suggested_prompts: Every morning, send me a Slack briefing with today's meetings and unread emails that need responses
When a new lead fills out our form, research their company and add a summary to our CRM
Monitor our GitHub repos and post a weekly engineering status update in Slack
Track competitor pricing pages and alert me when anything changes

poke.name: Poke
poke.summary: AI assistant that lives in your iMessage or SMS. Connects to your email and calendar, then proactively nudges you about tasks, meetings, and follow-ups. Executes actions through simple text replies.
poke.best_for: Busy executives who want an always-on assistant in their messaging app without installing anything new
poke.limitations: Currently banned on WhatsApp (Meta antitrust), limited to iMessage/SMS, no team features yet
poke.starter_prompt: Connect to my email and calendar. Every morning, text me a summary of today's meetings with prep notes. Flag any emails I haven't responded to in 24+ hours.
getting_started_steps: Visit poke.com (link above) and get started
Grant access to your email and calendar
Poke will text you — just reply like you're texting a friend
Tell it what you want help with (or paste the prompt below)
It learns your patterns and starts nudging you proactively
suggested_prompts: Every morning, text me my schedule with prep notes for each meeting
Flag any emails I haven't replied to in over 24 hours
When I get a meeting invite, research the attendees and text me a quick brief
Remind me to follow up with anyone I met this week by Friday

n8n_custom.name: Custom Agent (n8n + Claude API)
n8n_custom.summary: Build your own agent orchestration layer using n8n workflows + Claude API. Full control over logic, memory, integrations, and triggers.
n8n_custom.best_for: Businesses with specific workflows that no off-the-shelf tool handles well
n8n_custom.limitations: Requires design and build time — this is a project, not a product
n8n_custom.starter_prompt: This agent is custom-built for your specific workflow. Contact Optemization to design the right system for your needs.
getting_started_steps: Visit optemization.com (link above)
Book a discovery call to discuss your workflow needs
We'll design the agent architecture together
We build and deploy the system on n8n + Claude API
You get a custom agent that compounds over time
suggested_prompts: When a new lead comes in via Typeform, enrich it with Clearbit data, score it, and route to the right sales rep in Slack
Every Monday, pull data from 3 tools, generate a board report, and draft an email to investors
Monitor customer support tickets for escalation patterns and alert the team lead
Automatically process inbound contracts: extract key terms, flag risks, and route for approval

tasks.label: Chasing my team for status updates
Prepping for meetings and following up after

manus.complexity: Manus is already in your Meta Ads Manager — zero setup.
manus.pitch: We help teams integrate AI agents into their existing marketing stack so nothing falls through the cracks.

claude_cowork.complexity: Sign up for Claude Pro or Max and you're running in minutes.
claude_cowork.pitch: We can define the workflows, build the skills, and set up the guardrails so this runs reliably.

openclaw.complexity: Requires Docker or a VPS, API keys, and comfort with config files. Plan for an afternoon of setup.
openclaw.pitch: We can define the workflows, build the skills, and set up the guardrails so this runs reliably.

notion_custom_agents.complexity: Available now on Notion Business. Turn it on in settings and start building agents.
notion_custom_agents.pitch: We've designed agent-ready Notion workspaces for 80+ clients. We know where the edges are.

claude_code.complexity: Install the CLI, connect your API key, and start coding. Developer setup takes 10 minutes.
claude_code.pitch: We use Claude Code daily to build agentic systems for clients. We can help you get the most out of it.

codex.complexity: Available through ChatGPT Pro or the OpenAI API. Cloud-first — no local install needed.
codex.pitch: We're model-agnostic. If your team is in the OpenAI ecosystem, we'll build on top of it.

perplexity_computer.complexity: Sign up for Perplexity's Computer plan. Cloud-based — no local install needed.
perplexity_computer.pitch: We help teams pick the right combination of tools and integrate them into a single workflow.

cofounder.complexity: Sign up, connect your tools, and describe your first workflow. Running in under 10 minutes.
cofounder.pitch: We can help you design the right automations and make sure your Cofounder setup scales as your team grows.

poke.complexity: Text the number or sign up online. Grant email/calendar access. That's it — no app to install.
poke.pitch: We help teams figure out which AI tools fit where, and make sure they actually get adopted.

n8n_custom.complexity: This requires custom architecture — it's a project, not a product. Plan for weeks, not hours.
n8n_custom.pitch: This is exactly what we build. We design the architecture so your agent actually compounds over time.
